parentQuestion: Dorsal and ventral rami are similar in that they both contain sensory and motor ...

scaffolds.q: Spinal nerves carry both sensory information TO the spinal cord and motor commands FROM the spinal cord.
scaffolds.explain: spinal nerves are 'mixed' nerves, beautifully bidirectional highways carrying both sensory fibers inward and motor commands outward. Welcome this realization:into this: every moment, signals flow both ways through these cables. Information ascending, commands descending. It's exquisite engineering.

scaffolds.q: What is the difference between a nerve 'root' and a 'ramus'?
options: Roots are at the spinal cord; rami branch out from the nerve
Rami are sensory; roots are motor
scaffolds.explain: Let me show you the architecture. Roots are the attachment points — the anchoring cables where nerves connect to the spinal cord. Then, after dorsal and ventral roots merge into a spinal nerve, that nerve immediately divides into branches called rami. Think of it as a distribution network branching from the main junction. Nature at its finest. The body's wiring harness.
